*** 1 FATAL, 2 INJ *** AN EF3 TORNADO, STARTING AT 7:21 PM CDT 3/22/2022 NEAR 1 E HARVEY, TRACKED 11.5 MILES, ENDING AT 3 SSW EAST NEW ORLEANS AT 7:38 PM CDT. MAX WIND SPEED WAS 160 MPH AND THE MAX WIDTH WAS 320 YARDS. THIS IS THE STARTING POINT OF THE TORNADO TRACK. IN THE OFFICIAL NWS STORM DATA RECORD, THERE WILL BE INDIVIDUAL START/END POINTS FOR EACH PARISH. THIS IS THE ONLY LSR THAT WILL BE SENT PUBLICLY FOR THIS TORNADO. A FULL WRITE UP IS IN THE PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT.

NWS TORNADO SURVEY FOUND A TORNADO THAT STARTED NEAR BIG BRANCH MARSH NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE ON HIGHWAY 190. TORNADO CONTINUED NE CROSSING I-12 AND UP TO HIGHWAY 36. MAJORITY OF DAMAGE WAS TREES SNAPPED AND TREE/SHED DAMAGE. CONCLUDED INTENSITY OF EF1 90MPH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS WATER SPOUT REPORT FROM 1 ESE GOLDEN MEADOW. THE CARPORT DAMAGE IS FARTHER NORTH AND WILL BE STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. WE RECEIVED PICTURES OF A SMALL BOAT FLIPPED OVER ON THE WATER AND THE CAPTAIN SAID HE SAW A SMALL TORNADO, LIKELY A WATERSPOUT.

WE RECEIVED PICTURES OF A SMALL BOAT FLIPPED OVER ON THE WATER AND THE CAPTAIN SAID HE SAW A SMALL TORNADO, LIKELY A WATERSPOUT. A NEARBY TIN CARPORT ROOF THEN BLEW OFF, LIKELY FROM THE LANDSPOUT AS THE WATERSPOUT MOVED ONTO THE LAND. THIS COULD ALSO BE STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. RADAR IMAGERY DOES NOT SUPPORT ANYTHING STRONGER BUT IF MORE INFORMATION IS RECEIVED THIS WILL BE UPDATED.

UPDATE TO PREVIOUS REPORT BASED ON STORM SURVEY. SMALL AWNING SIMILAR TO A GAS STATION AWNING WAS LIFTED FROM THE GROUND AND THROWN INTO NEARBY POWER LINES. WEIGHT OF THE AWNING ON THE LINES CAUSED A FEW POLES TO BREAK. NO ADDITIONAL DAMAGE NOTED. MAY HAVE BEEN A VERY BRIEF TORNADO, BUT WITH ONLY ONE DAMAGE POINT, CANNOT CONFIRM IT AS SUCH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT. SECTION OF ROOF COLLAPSED ON GAUTIER MIDDLE SCHOOL. NO OBSERVED DAMAGE TO ANY SURROUNDING TREES OR BUILDINGS IN THE AREA. MAY HAVE BEEN A TORNADO, BUT WITH ONLY A SINGLE DAMAGE POINT IT CANNOT BE CONFIRMED AS SUCH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY OF THE MOSS POINT TORNADO FOUND DAMAGE REPRESENTATIVE OF AN EF-1. THE TORNADO TOUCHED NEAR GRIERSON ST AND HWY 63 IN JACKSON COUNTY, MS, WHERE IT SNAPPED HARDWOOD TREE TRUNKS. IT THEN CROSSED HWY 63 AND MARVIN AVE MOVING NE AND MOVED NORTHWARD OVER THE MARSH AREA AND CROSSED ELDER FERRY RD, WHERE IT SNAPPED A FEW TREE TRUNKS. IT CROSSED I-10 AND BROKE SOME BRANCHES OFF OF NEARBY TREES, IN ADDITION TO DAMAGING A ROAD SIGN. IT THEN LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER CROSSING I-10.
EM WATCHED TORNADO TOUCH DOWN IN MARSHY AREA NORTH OF DOWNTOWN MOSS POINT EAST OF HWY 513. ALSO RECEIVED PUBLIC REPORT OF VEHICLE AND BUILDING DAMAGE NEAR I-10.

UPDATE TO PREVIOUS REPORT BASED ON SURVEY. BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR RIVER WALK DR AND RIVER PLACE DR. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NOTED NEAR EAST END OF POLLY LAKE RD WHERE NUMEROUS LARGE PINES WERE SNAPPED. THEN MOVED NNW/N AND LIFTED AS IT APPROACHED INTERSECTION OF OLD RIVER RD AND MOUNGERS RD. AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO IN JACKSON COUNTY, MS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE SOCCER FIELDS AT LUM CUMBEST PARK, WHERE IT BENT FREESTANDING METAL LIGHT POLES. IT PROGRESSED NORTHWARD INTO THE PARK RIDGE ESTATES COMMUNITY, WHERE IT BROKE BRANCHES AND SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES. IT THEN PROGRESSED NORTHWARD INTO AN OPEN FIELD AND THEN LIFTED.
VIDEO OF TORNADO ON THE GROUND RELAYED TO NWS BY STORM CHASER.

A TORNADO OF UNKNOWN STRENGTH TOUCHED DOWN IN RURAL PORTIONS OF NORTHERN JACKSON COUNTY NEAR OR ALONG MISSISSIPPI HIGHWAY 63. THIS WAS CONFIRMED BY A STORM SPOTTER VIDEO OF A TORNADO RECEIVED IN THE AREA. BECAUSE NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED, THE TORNADO WILL BE RATED AS AN UNKNOWN STRENGTH. IF DAMAGE INFORMATION IS RECEIVE, THIS REPORT WILL BE UPDATED. DUE TO THIS DELAYED REPORT, NO PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT WILL BE ISSUED.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF HIGHWAY 90 AND JUST SOUTH OF BAYOU GAUCHE ROAD, PEELING THE ROOF OFF A HOUSE AND A CAR PORT AS WELL AS FLIPPING A RV. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED AROUND THE PROPERTY AND TO THE NORTHWEST AS WELL AS DEBRIS THROWN A SIGNIFICANT DISTANCE. NORTH OF BAYOU GAUCHE ROAD, A CHURCH COMPLEX HAD ROOF DAMAGE TO MOST OF THE BUILDINGS AS WELL AS A FLIPPED RV. THESE BUILDINGS WERE NOTED TO HAVE ALSO BEEN DAMAGED FROM IDA, WITH BLUE TARPS ON THE ROOFS STILL. ONE BRAND NEW ROOF, THOUGH, DID HAVE NOTABLE DAMAGE TO THE SOUTHEAST CORNER. AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHWEST ACROSS HIGHWAY 90, MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE AND LIGHT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, SUCH AS SHINGLES AND SIDING DAMAGE, WERE NOTED BEFORE THE TORNADO QUICKLY LIFTED.

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 250 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN ON PILGRIMS REST ROAD SOUTHEAST OF LIBERTY, MS AND ENDED ON CENTERVILLE ROAD, SOUTHEAST OF MAGNOLIA, MS. THE ENTIRE LENGTH OF THE TRACK SAW EXTENSIVE AND WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE CONSISTING OF LARGE UPROOTED TREES AND SNAPPED TREES NEAR THE BASE WITH MANY NOTABLE CONVERGENT SIGNATURES. ON KERMIT ROAD, A RESIDENTIAL HOUSE HAD PART OF ITS ROOF REMOVED ALONG WITH A DESTROYED BARN. THE BARN AND AN ADDITIONAL DAMAGED HOME WILL NEED TO BE ANALYZED VIA DRONE FOOTAGE. AS IT MOVED EAST, ON CHURCH ROAD, AN OLD BUILDING WAS DAMAGED. THESE LAST TWO LOCATIONS SAW THE MAX WIDTH OF THE TORNADO. ON JJ CARTER ROAD, THERE WAS EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A FEW MANUFACTURED HOMES. ONE WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION, ROTATED BACKWARDS AND ROLLED OVER AT LEAST ONCE. ANOTHER LOST ITS ENTIRE ROOF AND A WALL. THE OTHER HOMES HAD MINOR DAMAGE. DRONE DATA WILL BE NEEDED TO FILL IN DAMAGE BETWEEN HERE AND I-55 WHERE EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE HIGHWAY. IT MOVED EAST, DAMAGING A CAMP ON HIGHWAY 51 BEFORE LIFTING ON CENTERVILLE ROAD. DUE TO MANY ROADS BEING IMPASSABLE, DRONE DATA WILL BE ANALYZED SO TRACK WIDTH, LENGTH AND TORNADO STRENGTH MAY BE ADJUSTED. THANK YOU TO THE AMITE, PIKE AND MEMA EMERGENCY MANAGERS FOR THEIR HELP.

A TORNADO DROPPED DOWN NEAR CHAPPEPEELA ROAD, DAMAGING A SHED AT 12:10 PM CST. IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST, TEARING A SHED IN HALF AND THROWING IT DOWNSTREAM ABOUT 100 YARDS. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST, FLIPPING AND TURNING OVER A SHED AS IT REACHED AVERETT ROAD. A FEW SMALL TREES, LESS THAN 10 FEET TALL, WERE DOWNED AT THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST. IT TORN DOWN AND THREW SECTIONS OF RESIDENTIAL WOOD FENCING AS IT CROSSED BEAU PLACE LANE AND ALLEN DRIVE. ONE HOME ON ALLEN DRIVE HAD AROUND HALF A DOZEN HARDWOODS SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, CONSISTENT WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MPH. THE PATH WIDTH AT THIS POINT WAS THE GREATEST AT 50 YARDS. THE REST OF THE TORNADO TRACK WAS IN INACCESSIBLE WOODS AREA. VIDEO EVIDENCE SHOWED THE TORNADO TRACKING THROUGH THIS WOODED AND FIELD AREA NORTH OF HIGHWAY 40 AND WEST OF HIGHWAY 445. THE TORNADO LIFTED BEFORE REACHING HIGHWAY 445 AT APPROXIMATELY 12:18 PM CST.

*** 1 FATAL, 8 INJ *** CONFIRMED EF2 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 120 MPH, MAX WIDTH OF 75 YARDS, AND A LENGTH OF 5 MILES. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG THE PATH WITH A NUMBER OF OTHER HOMES RECEIVING MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE.

*** 6 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 125 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 175 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN IN JEFFERSON PARISH AND ENDED IN ST. BERNARD. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF AMES BLVD NEAR THE INTERSECTION WITH AUGUST AVE. RIGHT AFTER TOUCH DOWN, IT PUSHED A LARGE SET OF BLEACHERS OFF ITS FOUNDATION. WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE WAS ALSO OBSERVED HERE. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST INTO MARRERO AND HARVEY WHERE IT CAUSED WIDESPREAD STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALONG ITS PATH. A COUPLE HOMES IN HARVEY SUFFERED COMPLETE LOSS OF THEIR ROOFS WITH MULTIPLE WALLS COLLAPSED, AND WERE ALSO SHIFTED FROM THEIR FOUNDATIONS. OTHER HOMES ALONG THE PATH IN THIS AREA SUFFERED MINOR ROOF AND WALL DAMAGE. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST INTO GRETNA, CAUSING DAMAGE MOSTLY TO TREES. IT THEN STRENGTHENED AGAIN AS IT APPROACHED APPROACHED THE WESTBANK EXPRESSWAY IN FAR EASTERN GRETNA. IN THE VICINITY OF COOK ST AND THEARD ST, SEVERAL HOMES WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED, WITH PORTIONS OF ROOF STRUCTURE REMOVED, AND ONE HOME WAS COMPLETELY SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION. AFTER CROSSING THE WESTBANK EXPRESSWAY AND MOVING INTO ALGIERS, THE TORNADO AGAIN CAUSED SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALONG TITA ST. ONE HOME WAS SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND BEGAN TO ROLL OVER, BEING COMPLETELY DESTROYED IN THE PROCESS. THREE OTHERS SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT ROOF STRUCTURE LOSS OR PARTIAL WALL COLLAPSE. A CHURCH WAS ALSO SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED ON WILTZ LN. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED THE RIVER AND MOVED INTO ARABI. IT CONTINUED TO CAUSE DAMAGE TO TREES, POWER LINES AND STRUCTURES AS IT MOVED NORTHEASTWARD. THE TORNADO APPEARS TO HAVE LIFTED AFTER IT CROSSED PATRICIA ST. MINOR FACIA DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO AN APARTMENT BUILDING HERE, AND ONE TREE WAS SNAPPED IN AN OPEN FIELD ACROSS THE STREET. IT MAY HAVE CONTINUED INTO THE MARSH, BUT NO DAMAGE WAS OBSERVABLE BY THE SURVEY TEAM. THE MAXIMUM RATING OF 125 MPH EF-2 IS BASED ON THE HOMES THAT LOST ALL OR MOST OF THEIR ROOF STRUCTURE AS WELL AS SUFFERING SOME WALL COLLAPSE.
